method_atom: A proof technique showing that a discrete random combinatorial process defined on a fine mesh converges, as the mesh spacing vanishes (scaling limit), to a continuous random curve that is invariant under angle-preserving coordinate changes. The limit is identified with a one-parameter growth process generated by iterating conformal maps driven by a single one-dimensional Brownian motion (a stochastic Loewner-type evolution), which yields existence of the limit, its conformal invariance, and independence from the microscopic discretization (universality); a corollary is that such an evolution is almost surely traced by a continuous path.
object_atom: Planar loop-erased random walks and the associated uniform spanning trees on lattices inside simply connected or Jordan domains of the plane, together with the boundary-wired spanning-tree Peano (space-filling) curve joining two boundary points.
brief_reason: The scaling-limit-to-conformally-invariant-Loewner-evolution machinery and its universality argument carry across models and fields, while the loop-erased walks, spanning trees, and their Peano curve on planar lattices are the probability-theory-specific objects it is applied to.
